title: Lentivirus production & concentration
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e5sk9","text":"Lentivirus production protocol based on official protocol for Lipofectamine 3000 and TransIT-Lenti","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"3rahr","text":"Prepare culture media for HEK293T:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7j6o8","text":"  supplemented with 10%   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1},{"key":1,"offset":24,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"adqsu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"can_edit":false,"cas_number":"","guid":"41C56CF8341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","id":218325,"is_public":true,"mol_weight":0,"name":"DMEM, low glucose, GlutaMAX\u0026trade; Supplement, pyruvate","rrid":"","sku":"21885025","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/21885025","vendor":{"id":440,"image":{"placeholder":"/img/vendors/440.png","source":"/img/vendors/440.png"},"is_requested":false,"link":"https://www.thermofisher.com","name":"Thermo Fisher"}}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":241911,"guid":"420118E9341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Fetal Bovine Serum, qualified, One Shot\u0026trade; format, Brazil","mol_weight":0,"sku":"A3160801","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/A3160801","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":440,"name":"Thermo Fisher","link":"https://www.thermofisher.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/440.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/440.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Detach, count & seed HEK293T cells into T75 flasks at: 4 x 10E6 cells 6 x 10E6 cells 8 x 10E6 cells 10 x 10E6 cells >28 x 10E6 cells required in total; recommend detaching cells from 1x T175 at 70-80% confluence.

Incubate plates at 37 °C, incubator (5% CO2, humidified) for up to 24:00:00 Observe confluence of cells under a microscope at 18 - 24 hours after plating. The optimal plating density for transfection yield 80-90% confluence 18 - 24 hours after plating.

Detach, count & seed HEK293T cells at a density that yields 80-90% confluence 18 - 24 hours after plating Incubate at 37 °C, incubator (5% CO2, humidified) for up to 24:00:00 Lentivirus packaging medium (step 7 below) can be used when plating cells & during transfection the next day, but DMEM+10% FBS works well too

When HEK293T cells are at 80-90% confluence, transfect cells with lentiviral transfer and packaging plasmids using lipofectamine 3000 Table 1. If transfecting multple vessels, make a master mix of 1.1x the total volume required Source document: Lipofectamine3000-LentiVirus-AppNote-Global-FHR.pdf

Make up lipofectamine mix by diluting Lipofectamine 3000 (7ul / 3ug DNA) into Opti-MEM (volume specified in Table 1) Mix well by vortexing 00:00:03 Lipofectamine 3000 reagent diluted in Opti-MEM medium should be used within 15 minutes of dilution. Longer times can result in a loss of transfection efficiency

Combine both lipofectamine and DNA mixes and incubate for 00:20:00 Room temperature to allow DNA-lipid complexes to form Remove 50% volume of media from HEK293T culture vessels intended for transfection A B C D E 1 Volume of media to remove per vessel (ml) 2 6-well 100mm dish T75 150mm dish T175 3 1 5 7.5 15 17.5
Make up DNA mix by diluting transfer and packaging plasmids into Opti-MEM, then adding P3000 reagent (2ul / 1ug DNA) Mix well by vortexing 00:00:03
Add 1 µL LentiFuge Cellecta Catalog #LFVC1 per 1 mL, lentiviral supernatant Incubate at 4 °C 01:00:00
Detach, count & seed HEK293T cells at a density that yields 80-90% confluence 18 - 24 hours after plating Incubate at 37 °C, incubator (5% CO2, humidified) for up to 24:00:00

Gently add DNA-lipid complexes to cells A B C D E 1 Volume of DNA-lipid complex to add per vessel (ml) 2 6-well 10cm dish T75 150mm dish T175 3 0.5 3 4 8 9 Gently rock culture vessel back-and-forth and from side-to-side to evenly distribute Incubate at 37 °C, incubator (5% CO2, humidified) for 06:00:00

5-6 hours after transfection, replace media with pre-warmed packaging medium Incubate plates at 37 °C, incubator (5% CO2, humidified) Consider supplementation with 1 millimolar (mM), sodium butyrate or 2 millimolar (mM), caffeine to improve titer. Supplements will be present in viral supernatant if not concentrated

Prepare Lentivirus packaging medium (Opti-MEM + 1x GlutaMAX + 1mM sodium pyruvate + 5% FBS) Supplement 500 mL Opti-MEM™ I Reduced Serum Medium Thermo Fisher Catalog #31985047 with: 5 mL GlutaMAX™ Supplement Thermo Fisher Catalog #35050038 5 mL Sodium Pyruvate (100 mM) Thermo Fisher Catalog #11360070 25 mL Fetal Bovine Serum, qualified, One Shot™ format, Brazil Thermo Fisher Catalog #A3160801

Calculate the amount of each plasmid required Example using lentiCas9-Blast, psPAX2 and pMD2.G: A B C D E F G H 1 Mass of plasmids per vessel (ug) 2 Plasmid Size (bp) Equimolar ratio (ug) 6-well 10cm T75 150mm T175 3 lentiCas9-Blast 12859 2.209 1.313 7.88 10.50 21.00 23.63 4 psPAX2 10709 1.839 1.093 6.56 8.75 17.49 19.68 5 pMD2.G 5822 1.000 0.594 3.57 4.75 9.51 10.70 6 7 Total 3 18 24 48 54 Optimisation of plasmid ratios may be required for each transfer plasmid; an equimolar ratio is a good starting point. Zhang Lab uses a 4:3:2 transfer : psPAX2 : pMD2.G ratio that approximates an equimolar ratio for lentiCas9-blast, lentiGuide-Puro & lentciCRISPR v2 plasmids. With lentiCas9-blast, 4:2:1, 9:8:1, 7.5:1.5:4 ratios all result in similar titres (+/- 20% of 4:3:2), around 1-2e6 TU/ml.

48-52 hours post-transfection, collect supernatant from culture vessels and combine with supernatant harvested the day before. Centrifuge at 300 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes to separate any detached cells, and collect the supernatant.
Filter the supernatant through a 0.45μm PES filter to remove any remaining cellular debris Smaller filters (0.22μm) improve purity but can lower the viral titer.

When HEK293T cells are at 80-90% confluence, transfect cells with lentiviral transfer and packaging plasmids using TransIT-Lenti If transfecting multple vessels, make a master mix of 1.1x the total volume required

Calculate the amount of each plasmid required Example using lentiCas9-Blast, psPAX2 and pMD2.G: A B C D E F G H 1 Mass of plasmids per vessel (ug) 2 Plasmid Size (bp) Equimolar ratio (ug) 6-well 10cm T75 150mm T175 3 lentiCas9-Blast 12859 2.209 0.875 5.25 6.56 13.13 15.31 4 psPAX2 10709 1.839 0.729 4.37 5.47 10.93 12.75 5 pMD2.G 5822 1.000 0.396 2.38 2.97 5.94 6.93 6 7 Total 2 12 15 30 35 Optimisation of plasmid ratios is required for each transfer plasmid but an equimolar ratio is a good starting point, Zhang Lab uses a 4:3:2 ratio that approximates an equimolar ratio for lentiCas9-blast, lentiGuide-Puro & lentciCRISPR v2 plasmids.

Combine plasmids in quantities determined in step 14.1 Add Opti-MEM (Volume specified in Table 2), mix by pipetting Add TransIT reagent (3ul / 1ug DNA), mix by pipetting Incubate at Room temperature 00:10:00 to allow transfection complexes to form Precipitation may be observed when excess DNA is used during complex formation. This may negatively impact transfection efficiency.

Add transfection copmlexes drop-wise to different areas of vessel Gently agitate culture vessel back-and-forth and from side-to-side to evenly distribute Incubate at 37 °C, incubator (5% CO2, humidified) for 48:00:00

24 hours post-transfection, collect supernatant from culture vessels and store at 4 °C Replace media with pre-warmed packaging medium Incubate plates at 37 °C, incubator (5% CO2, humidified) for up to 28:00:00
Harvest virus 48 hours post-transfection by following steps 10 - 12.

Prepare single-use aliquots in cryovials and snap freeze virus in a dry ice-ethanol bath, then store at -80°C. Alternatively, proceed with virus concentration or infection of cells. It is recommended to avoid subjecting lentiviral preparations to multiple freeze-thaw cycles, since each cycle can result in a 10%–20% loss in functional titers.
Warm TransIT-Lenti reagent to room temperature and vortex 00:00:03

Centrifuge at 12000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 hour Pellets generated from small supernatant volumes may be difficult to see. It is advised to mark the centrifuge tube or bottle with a marker at the site where you expect the virus pellet to be.
Resuspend the pelleted lentiviral preparation in 1/100 of original volume using sterile phosphate buffered saline (PBS).
Prepare single-use aliquots and snap freeze virus in dry ice, then store at -80°C.
Change media for HEK293T cells
creator: Allan JW Lui
